title: Is air conditioning essential for coping with heatwaves? France might be changing its mind on the issue
subhed: While more and more people are installing air conditioning, this technology is still seen by many as a misguided response to climate change. Some experts have challenged that argument as France faces unusually high temperatures.

title: French presidential candidates capitalize on social media, bypassing journalists

German government stokes up the memory wars surrounding the 'displaced persons' of 1945
subhed: Germany is marking World Refugee Day on Saturday, commemorating the 12 million Germans expelled from Central and Eastern Europe after World War II. Under pressure from the far right, the Christian Democratic government has taken up the issue and chosen to assign it to the Ministry of the Interior rather than the Ministry of Culture.

title: Thomas Piketty: 'To clarify its own stance and unite its ranks, the left must propose a referendum to adopt a national solidarity tax'
subhed: As the 2027 presidential campaign draws nearer in France, left-wing parties should take inspiration from a 1945 bill and propose a referendum on imposing a more stringent tax on the wealthy, writes economist Thomas Piketty. He believes up to €800 billion could be raised.

title: Fête de la Musique: Our picks for concerts not to miss
subhed: Le Monde's Culture desk offers its selection of concerts taking place on Sunday, June 21, as part of the national Fête de la Musique, which has become a global phenomenon.

title: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
subhed: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhed: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

title: Mozart manuscript discovered at French National Library to be performed for the first time
subhed: The music notebook, found in the archives, dates from the composer's final stay in Paris, in 1778. The seven short pieces for flute and harp it contains will be broadcast for the first time ever on June 22 on France Musique radio.

title: Wealthy taxpayers clean out their holdings, keeping their homes, sports cars and gold bars safe
subhed: The new 20% tax on 'luxury' assets held in personal holdings is prompting owners to quickly remove them. As a result, the measure risks bringing in almost nothing.
